channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Lego Skylines Leaked by Korean Rating Board
item.content:encoded:
As seems to happen every year when we get near the summer announcement events, some games get leaked by rating boards posting information before reveals at events. One of this year's victims is Paradox Interactive and their previously unannounced game Lego Skylines.

This would presumably be a spinoff of the Cities: Skylines series done in a Lego aesthetic. While Paradox Interactive is listed as the publisher, we don't have any developer listed in this case. The original Cities: Skylines was developed by Colossal Order, who also developed the sequel until parting ways with Paradox late last year, after which Iceflake Studios took over development of the troubled sequel. Whether either of those teams, or in fact any internal Paradox team, would be developing the game is unknown at this time.

One imagines that a Lego Skylines product would draw heavily on the look of various Lego City sets and is likely to be more inclined towards play than strict simulation as much. It would be the latest of a push by Lego into video games over the past few years which has seen games like Lego Party, Lego Voyagers, Lego Horizon Adventures, Lego Fortnite, and most recently Lego Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight. 

First spotted by Gematsu, the rating board also included announcements regarding Persona 4: Revival a previously announced Persona 4 remake that many have been awaiting details on. Questions here include whether it will include the Persona 4 Golden material, as the remake of Persona 3 did not include all of the content in updated releases, and if there are any plans to perhaps stop Teddy from being such a pervert in the game. 

We can expect to hear more about some of these titles in the coming weeks at Summer Game Fest, and similar events that will be taking place in early June.

A longtime lover of speculative fiction, in almost all its forms, Don joined TechRaptor in 2014 on a whim sending in an application as he was looking for… More about Don
